mission.title: Install and Configure MongoDB Community Operator
mission.description: The MongoDB Community Operator allows you to deploy and manage MongoDB Community instances on Kubernetes. It simplifies the management of MongoDB clusters, but it is deprecated and users are encouraged to migrate to the new MongoDB Kubernetes Operator. Key trade-offs include limited support and the need to transition to a new repository in the near future.

steps.title: Add the MongoDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the MongoDB Community Operator Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add mongodb https://mongodb.github.io/mongodb-kubernetes-operator

```

> This adds the correct repository for the MongoDB Community Operator.

steps.title: Install the MongoDB Community Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the MongoDB Community Operator in the specified namespace.

```bash

helm install mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --create-namespace --version 0.13.0

```

> Ensure the namespace is created if it doesn't exist.

steps.title: Create a MongoDB custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource definition (CRD) for MongoDB, which defines the desired state of your MongoDB deployment.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.5
  persistent: true
EOF

```

> This creates a MongoDB replica set with 3 members.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.5
  persistent: true
EOF

steps.title: Verify the MongoDB deployment
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the MongoDB custom resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb

```

> You should see the status of your MongoDB instance.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the MongoDB Community Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the MongoDB Community Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all MongoDB data and resources created by the operator.

```bash

helm uninstall mongodb-community-operator --namespace mongodb

kubectl delete namespace mongodb

```

> This command removes the operator.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the MongoDB Community Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your MongoDB data. This process will upgrade the operator and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb -o yaml > mongodb-backup.yaml

helm upgrade mongodb-community-operator mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator --namespace mongodb --version 0.13.0

kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb

```

> Backup the current MongoDB custom resource.
> This command upgrades the operator to the specified version.
> Verify that the MongoDB instance is running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb -o yaml > mongodb-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'MongoDB CRD not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MongoDB custom resource definition has not been installed.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/mongodb/mongodb-kubernetes-operator/main/deploy/crds/mongodb.com_mongodb_crd.yaml' to install the CRD.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'MongoDB instance is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MongoDB pods may not be running due to insufficient resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n mongodb' and ensure your cluster has enough resources.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to MongoDB'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MongoDB service may not be exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is created with 'kubectl get svc -n mongodb' and check the service configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The MongoDB Community Operator may not be installed.

**Fix:**
Run 'helm list --namespace mongodb' to check if the operator is installed.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kubernetes 1.30 and improved logging capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of legacy MongoDB configurations.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.11, ensure to migrate your custom resources to the new format as per the migration guide.

resolution.summary: You should see the MongoDB instance listed with 'kubectl get mongodb -n mongodb' showing the status as 'Ready'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: mongodb.com/v1
kind: MongoDB
metadata:
  name: my-mongo
  namespace: mongodb
spec:
  members: 3
  type: ReplicaSet
  version: 5.0.5
  persistent: true
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
